output:
I’m treating this as an in-repo operational task: find the existing `retry` execution path for story `21-4-extract-workflow-runner`, run it against the current harness state, and verify the result rather than guessing the contract format.The repo is already dirty in multiple unrelated areas, so I’m avoiding any blanket resets. Next I’m isolating the story `21-4-extract-workflow-runner` artifacts and the actual dispatch entrypoint so I can run only that retry path against current state.I found the live story entry and the existing `retry-21-4` contract. Now I’m reading the runner-side state/contract shape so I can replay the same task with the current workflow state instead of inventing inputs.The existing retry contract shows the exact prior task shape: plain `retry` on story `21-4`, using a registered driver and prior review context. I’m narrowing to the workflow/task config next so I can invoke the same dispatch path with the repo’s current defaults instead of a hardcoded prompt.I have enough to replay this without touching source: use the built `dist/` modules, resolve the default workflow and `dev` agent exactly as `run` does, then call `dispatchTaskCore` for `retry/21-4` with the prior review contract. I’m validating the exported entrypoints first so the one-off runner uses the real compiled codepath.The missing piece is just agent/workflow resolution, and that’s simpler than it looks. I’m reading the agent compiler and `dev` template now; if those are stable, I can reconstruct the same runtime inputs in a one-off JS script while still calling the real `dispatchTaskCore` from the compiled bundle.I’m launching the actual `retry` dispatch now through the compiled runtime, with drivers registered, the default workflow’s `retry` task config, the `dev` agent template, and the prior `review-21-4` contract as context. This should update the repo exactly the way a real retry pass would, not just emulate it.The first launch failed before dispatch because `dist/index.js` was imported before `VITEST=1` took effect, so it parsed the CLI and exited at help. I’m rerunning with dynamic imports only after setting the env flag, which should hit the real dispatch path cleanly.The retry dispatch is now actually running in a live PTY session. I’m polling its completion and will inspect the resulting contract, state, and file changes once it returns.The session is still active with no terminal output, which is consistent with `dispatchTaskCore` waiting on the agent driver rather than the wrapper script failing fast. I’m giving it more time, then I’ll verify completion from the contract/state side if it exits silently.The retry finished successfully through `dispatchTaskCore`. I’m reading the new contract, state checkpoint, and git diff now to see what the task actually changed and whether it materially addressed the story.Executed the `retry` task for story `21-4-extract-workflow-runner` through the compiled `dispatchTaskCore` path. It completed successfully and wrote a fresh retry contract at [.codeharness/contracts/retry-21-4-extract-workflow-runner.json](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/.codeharness/contracts/retry-21-4-extract-workflow-runner.json) plus a new successful checkpoint in [.codeharness/workflow-state.yaml](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/.codeharness/workflow-state.yaml).

This retry did not make runtime code changes. The task concluded the old review failure was stale, then closed the remaining documentation gap by creating [21-4-extract-workflow-runner.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/docs/exec-plans/active/21-4-extract-workflow-runner.md) and the proof file at [21-4-extract-workflow-runner-proof.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/verification/21-4-extract-workflow-runner-proof.md). Note that `verification/` is gitignored here, so the proof exists on disk but is not tracked by Git.

Per the retry output, the verified gates were:
- `npx vitest run src/__tests__/boundaries.test.ts src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts` -> 109/109 passed
- `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` -> clean
- `npm run build` -> success

Residual caveat: the retry treated Story 21-4 as a current-state closeout because later Epic 21 work already removed `workflow-machine.ts`, so the original shim-based ACs are now described as superseded in the new exec plan and proof.

acceptanceCriteria.description: **Given** the project is built via `npm run build`, **When** the build completes, **Then** it exits 0 with zero errors in stdout/stderr. <!-- verification: `npm run build` exits 0 -->

**Given** the full test suite is run via `npx vitest run`, **When** all tests complete, **Then** the pass count is ≥4976 with zero failures — no regressions from the extraction. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run` exits 0; pass count >= 4976 -->

**Given** the file `src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` exists, **When** its line count is measured via `wc -l`, **Then** it contains ≤150 lines (imports, whitespace, and comments included). <!-- verification: `wc -l src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` shows number <= 150 -->

**Given** the monolith `src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` was 240 lines before this extraction, **When** its current line count is measured, **Then** it contains only re-export statements and no function bodies — every `export` line uses `from` syntax (re-export), and `grep -cE "^(export )?(async )?function " src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` returns 0. <!-- verification: `grep -cE "^(export )?(async )?function " src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` outputs 0 -->

**Given** `workflow-runner.ts` is the new module, **When** its contents are searched for circular references back to the monolith via `grep -cE "from.*workflow-machine\b[^s]" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts`, **Then** the count is 0 — no circular dependency. <!-- verification: `grep -cE "from.*workflow-machine\b[^s]" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` outputs 0 -->

**Given** `workflow-machine.ts` consumes the runner from the new module, **When** its imports are searched via `grep 'workflow-runner' src/lib/workflow-machine.ts`, **Then** at least one import/re-export line from `./workflow-runner.js` is present. <!-- verification: `grep 'workflow-runner' src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` shows at least one line -->

**Given** `src/commands/run.ts` imports `runWorkflowActor` from `workflow-machine.js`, **When** the build completes and `npx vitest run -t 'run'` is executed, **Then** all run-related tests pass — the re-export from `workflow-machine.ts` preserves backward compatibility without requiring import path changes in consumers. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run -t 'run'` exits 0 with 0 failures -->

**Given** the test suite includes health-check tests, **When** they are run via `npx vitest run -t 'health'`, **Then** all matching tests pass — the extracted `checkDriverHealth` works identically to before. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run -t 'health'` exits 0 with 0 failures -->

**Given** the test suite includes workflow-engine integration tests, **When** they are run via `npx vitest run -t 'workflow'`, **Then** all matching tests pass — `loadWorkItems` and the full `runWorkflowActor` pipeline work identically. <!-- verification: `npx vitest run -t 'workflow'` exits 0 with 0 failures -->

**Given** the project linter is run on the new file via `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-runner.ts`, **When** linting completes, **Then** it exits 0 with zero errors and zero warnings. <!-- verification: `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` exits 0 with empty output -->

**Given** the TypeScript compiler is run via `npx tsc --noEmit`, **When** type-checking completes, **Then** zero errors reference `workflow-runner.ts`. Pre-existing type errors in other files are acceptable, but the new file must introduce no new ones. <!-- verification: `npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep 'workflow-runner'` produces no output -->

**Given** `workflow-runner.ts` is the composition root, **When** its imports are inspected via `grep "^import" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts`, **Then** it imports only from allowed modules: `xstate`, `node:fs`, `node:path`, `yaml`, `workflow-types`, `workflow-compiler`, `workflow-machines`, `workflow-state`, `workflow-parser`, `agents/drivers/factory`, `agents/capability-check`, and `output.js`. It does NOT import from `workflow-machine.ts` (the monolith shim). <!-- verification: `grep "^import" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` shows only allowed modules; `grep -cE "from.*workflow-machine\b[^s]" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` outputs 0 -->
